properties.description: Hungarians are heading to the polls on Sunday in an election that will potentially end Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule. properties.title: What Trump’s blowup with Rutte was really about

But while scanning becomes illegal, tech firms aren’t likely to stop the practice just yet. A temporary European Union legislation has allowed companies — including Meta, Google, Microsoft and Snapchat — to voluntarily scan their services for child sexual abuse material (CSAM). Law enforcement officials have described the process as “vital” to crack down on child abuse. But the regime ran out on April 3, and EU legislators last month failed to agree on the terms to extend it.
